In-house electrical capability via Durabilis Electrical & Maintenance Ltd — potentially useful for a project where electrical and fit-out coordination matters.
Project overview shared in enquiry: strip-out, two clinical treatment rooms in metal stud, plumbing provision, electrical circuits, clinical vinyl flooring with welded seams and coved skirting, HVAC extension, full repaint including antimicrobial micropaint. Family member handling joinery, cabinetry and painting. Conservation area unit. CQC-registered clinic — clinical finish standards non-negotiable. Fit-out start early September 2026, opening 1 November 2026. Formal tender to follow once architect appointed and drawings produced post-lease exchange.
Status: Enquiry sent — awaiting response.

column4: SM:5 Developments Ltd — £TBC (VAT status TBC)
column9:
Principal contractor — 9A Jewry Street fit-out. Initial enquiry sent to info@sm5developments.co.uk. Southampton-based design-and-build and fit-out specialist, incorporated 2018, registered in England and Wales 11629923. Healthcare listed as a named sector alongside office, education, leisure and automotive. Full service capability: build, fit-out, refurbishment, bespoke furniture, maintenance and aftercare. Professionally structured firm — CHAS/Constructionline accreditations likely given sector positioning.
Engaged as a top-end benchmark rather than a primary appointment target. Overhead structure and project scale calibration likely places them above budget for this scope — expected quote range £45,000–£70,000+ ex VAT for the trimmed contractor scope. Value is in establishing a credentialled healthcare upper benchmark against which smaller contractor quotes can be validated.
Project overview shared in enquiry: strip-out, two clinical treatment rooms in metal stud, plumbing provision, electrical circuits, clinical vinyl flooring with welded seams and coved skirting, HVAC extension, full repaint including antimicrobial micropaint. Family member handling joinery, cabinetry and painting. Conservation area unit. CQC-registered clinic — clinical finish standards non-negotiable. Fit-out start early September 2026, opening 1 November 2026. Formal tender to follow once architect appointed and drawings produced post-lease exchange.
Status: Enquiry sent — awaiting response. May decline to quote on scale grounds.

column4: Richard Witcher — Witcher Crawford Architects — £TBC (VAT standard rate assumed)
column9:
Lead consultant / architectural services — 9A Jewry Street. Positive response to full scope brief. Richard confirmed Witcher Crawford are happy to provide a fixed fee covering all items on the brief including measured survey, space planning, Building Regulations, tender package, contractor procurement, contract administration, payment certification and variation control. Full scope coverage in a single appointment — no outsourcing caveats unlike Bressummer.
Richard has requested a call Monday morning to discuss level of internal finish required from them specifically around joinery, floor and wall finishes — sensible question, shows he's read the brief and is thinking about scope boundaries rather than just saying yes to everything. This is a positive professional signal.
Brochure shared covering disabled access and commercial care fit-out projects — relevant adjacent experience to a clinical environment. References offered once Monday call completed.
Winchester High Street based — genuine local practice, conservation area familiarity is a real advantage for Jewry Street. RIBA and ARB registered — strongest architectural credential of all firms contacted. Most likely to carry weight with Winchester City Council planning and Building Control if any issues arise.
Key unknowns still to resolve on Monday: fee level, programme, depth of clinical/healthcare experience, approach to Building Regulations detail, contractor usability of drawings, involvement during construction phase.
Call Monday morning confirmed — fee proposal to follow.
Status: Monday call booked. Strong early impression — full scope confidence, local knowledge, right credentials. Awaiting fee proposal before ranking against Bressummer and others.

column4: Hannah Arkell BA (Hons) MArch ARB RIBA — Studio Arkell Ltd — £5,400 + VAT (£6,480 inc VAT) — 9A Jewry Street SO23 8RZ Concept Design, Planning and Building Regulations Package (VAT standard rate)
column9: Fully qualified RIBA and ARB registered architect — strongest architectural credential of all firms reviewed. Based in Christchurch, Dorset. Quote ref ABI01CONTRACT1 dated 2 June 2026, valid 30 days. Three-stage fixed fee: Stage 1 Concept Design (survey, existing drawings, proposed drawings, design review, one amended set) £2,300 + VAT. Stage 2 Planning (planning pack, submission, council liaison) £1,200 + VAT. Stage 3 Building Regulations (GA plans at 1:50, fire strategy review, elevations, drainage site plan, specification notes, submission and building control liaison) £1,900 + VAT. Total £5,400 + VAT. Professional indemnity confirmed with Alan Boswell Insurance Brokers. Payment terms 50% upfront per stage, balance on completion. Correctly identified Use Class E position — no planning required for internal works, advertisement consent required for shopfront in conservation area. Building Regulations submission included in fee — positive differentiator versus Parkers who produce drawings only. Stage 3 commences after planning approval — potential programme risk if advertisement consent is delayed. Amendment allowance limited to 6 hours drawing amendments and council liaison — anything beyond charged at hourly rate. One site visit included, additional visits £60/hour plus 55p/mile from Christchurch BH23. Builder introductions available through Hannah's network. Appointment under RIBA Standard Professional Services Contract 2020. Does not include tender management, JCT contract or construction phase services — these would need Derek (Bressummer) or self-managed. Key risk: amendment cap could create additional cost exposure on a project where layout is still being refined. Recommend confirming whether amendment allowance can be expanded within fixed fee before instructing. Status: Strongest architectural option reviewed. Strong candidate alongside Derek for combined design and project management model.

column4: Derek Johnson BSc (Hons) FRICS — Bressummer A.R.K. Limited — Lead Consultant — £8,540 + VAT (£10,248 inc VAT) — 9A Jewry Street SO23 8RZ Full Lead Consultancy Package including Principal Designer (VAT standard rate)
column9: Full lead consultant service covering the entire project lifecycle from Schedule of Condition through to practical completion at 9A Jewry Street, Winchester. FRICS qualified Director personally undertaking all work. RICS regulated firm based at Hursley, Winchester — genuinely local. Did Schedule of Condition for 5 Boroughs Pizza at 10 Jewry Street next door — directly relevant local experience. Also currently tendering 8 Andover Road Winchester retail refurbishment (£55k) and completed Postmark Place Eastleigh CAT A+ retail fit-out (£104k August 2025). Fee breakdown: Schedule of Condition £940, Measured Survey £650, Design and Specification £2,450, Tender Stage £1,650, Construction Phase £165/hour capped at 10 hours (£1,650 maximum), Principal Designer £1,200 — total £8,540 + VAT. All pre-contract stages invoiced on completion of each stage. Construction phase invoiced monthly. Payment terms 14 days from invoice. Construction phase revised from Issue 1 (£2,250/month) to ad hoc hourly with fee cap following client negotiation — material improvement. Pre-start meeting and practical completion final inspection included within construction phase cap. Principal Designer covers CDM 2015 duties — Derek confirmed honest caveat on Building Safety Act experience but this is irrelevant for a single-storey commercial fit-out which remains firmly under CDM 2015 jurisdiction. Scope explicitly excludes: specialist investigations (asbestos, drainage, M&E testing, structural engineering), occupational risk assessments (fire, legionella), statutory application fees (building control, planning — paid by client as disbursements). Derek liaises with building control and manages planning/advertisement consent applications within his fee. Timeline from instruction: measured survey and spec 2 weeks, tender period 4 weeks, tender report and award 2 weeks, contractor lead-in 2-3 weeks, construction 4-6 weeks. Biggest programme risk flagged proactively: pre-construction activities must commence with Carter Jonas still in situ. Status: Strongly recommended for appointment. Decision required on whether to use alongside Parkers for architectural drawings or as standalone consultant. Issue 2 dated 2 June 2026.

column4: Matthew Jensen BA (Hons) AssocRINA — 1FRL Limited — £TBC (indicative ranges only, no formal quote yet) — 9A Jewry Street SO23 8RZ Architectural Services
column9: Winchester-based architectural practice at Alresford, Hampshire — genuinely local. Managing Director responding personally. AssocRINA qualified — Associate of the Royal Institution of Naval Architects, not RIBA. Worth noting this is a naval architecture qualification, not a building architecture one, though the firm clearly operates as a building design practice. Company registered England, No. 11559545. Indicative fee ranges provided without site visit — not a formal quote. Measured Building Survey/3D Laser Scan £1,100 + VAT. Planning Submission (external signage/change of use) £4,000–£5,000 + VAT to submission only, does not include acting as agent — significantly more expensive than Parkers for planning element and explicitly excludes agent role. Construction Drawing Package RIBA Stage 4 only £2,000–£5,000 + VAT — wide range, unanchored without site visit. Building Regulations Coordination and Submission £2,000–£3,000 + VAT. Consulting/design hourly rate £100 + VAT. Total indicative range excluding planning: £5,100–£9,100 + VAT. Total including planning: £9,100–£14,100 + VAT — materially more expensive than Parkers at £2,419 + VAT for equivalent scope. Key flag: planning submission quoted at £4,000–£5,000 but we have already confirmed no planning permission required for internal works — only advertisement consent for frontage. If that's what they're pricing, it's significantly over the odds. Building Regulations coordination and submission included in fee which is positive — Parkers do not submit, only produce drawings. Does not include specialist surveys, other consultants or clinical approval requirements. Offers to start measured survey immediately. Status: Expensive relative to Parkers and Derek on a like-for-like basis. No formal quote issued. Not recommended to pursue further unless both Parkers and Derek fall through.You said: give a resposne

column4: Ben Towers (Associate Director) — Butler Signs — £1,953.00 + VAT (£2,343.60 inc VAT) — Quote 14671, expires 25/06/2026 — 9A Jewry Street SO23 8RZ Signage Package (VAT standard rate)
column9:
Signage package covering four items. Fascia lettering £814 — flat-cut 3mm aluminium, matt black, brass locators, "ABI PETERS" at 200mm, "SKIN CLINIC" at 125mm, "MEDICAL GRADE SKINCARE & AESTHETICS" at 60mm. Window vinyl £64 — frost applied internally, contour-cut black vinyl externally, estimated 2,550mm x 1,000mm. Door vinyl £45 — frost internally, contour-cut black vinyl externally, 650mm x 1,780mm. Projecting sign £640 — aluminium tray 600mm x 450mm x 50mm, stock RAL finish, vinyl graphics, black projecting iron bracket. Installation £390 — includes travel and erection at working height under 4m. Site survey and digital proof included at no charge. Payment terms COD with 40% downpayment (£937.44). Quote expires 25 June 2026. Based in Newbury, Berkshire — not local but installs on site. Excludes removal of existing signage, out-of-hours working, parking charges. All installation subject to site inspection. Registered company Cardan Commerce Limited, VAT registered.
Comparing directly to Mint Signs at £1,908.14 + VAT (£2,289.77 inc VAT) — Butler is £44.86 + VAT more expensive on headline price. Mint is currently cheaper on a like-for-like basis though scope differs slightly — Mint quoted a framed hanging panel versus Butler's aluminium tray projecting sign, and Mint included solid frosted etch backing on window vinyl. Worth comparing spec detail before deciding. Conservation area flag applies to both — advertisement consent required before any external signage is installed. Do not instruct either firm until consent confirmed. Status: Competitive quote, second to Mint on price. Hold pending advertisement consent confirmation and final scope comparison against Mint.
